message:
chore(beads): sync .beads/issues.jsonl with current issue state

Update the beads JSONL export to reflect the latest issue metadata:
content_hash recomputations from the normalization ordering fix, updated
timestamps on issues touched by MCP input validation changes, and
refreshed dependency/comment payloads from recent sync operations.

This is a routine beads database sync — no issue content was manually
edited.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Scope
- Create a reusable test workspace helper that spins up temp repos, initializes .beads, and isolates runs.

- Implement artifact logging: JSONL event log per command, full stdout/stderr capture, file tree snapshots + diffs (.beads + repo root), and a structured summary per test.

Acceptance
- Common helper API usable by all test modules.
- Logs stored under target/test-artifacts/<suite>/<test>/ with deterministic filenames.
- On failure, artifacts are preserved; on success, logs are retained but minimal.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:40:23.716406794Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:03:24.855407306Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:03:24.855407306Z","close_reason":"Harness foundation already implemented.  {"id":"beads_rust-7xy","title":"Implement shell completions for bash/zsh/fish","description":"# Shell Completions Implementation

## Purpose
Provide tab completion for br commands, flags, and arguments across all major shells. This is a **critical UX feature** that dramatically improves usability.

## Why This Matters
- Tab completion is EXPECTED for modern CLI tools
- Reduces typing and cognitive load
- Helps users discover commands and flags
- Prevents typos in issue IDs and command names
- Professional polish that signals quality

## Technical Requirements

### Supported Shells
- **bash** - Most common on Linux servers
- **zsh** - Default on macOS, popular on Linux
- **fish** - Modern shell with excellent completion

### Completion Features
1. **Command completion**: `br <TAB>` shows all commands
2. **Flag completion**: `br list --<TAB>` shows flags for list
3. **Issue ID completion**: `br show bd-<TAB>` completes issue IDs
4. **Status completion**: `br update --status <TAB>` shows valid statuses
5. **Priority completion**: `br create -p <TAB>` shows 0-4
6. **Type completion**: `br create --type <TAB>` shows task/bug/feature/etc.

### Implementation with clap
```rust
// In build.rs or dedicated generator
use clap_complete::{generate_to, shells::{Bash, Zsh, Fish}};

fn main() {
    let outdir = std::path::Path::new("completions");
    let mut cmd = build_cli();
    
    generate_to(Bash, &mut cmd, "br", outdir).unwrap();
    generate_to(Zsh, &mut cmd, "br", outdir).unwrap();
    generate_to(Fish, &mut cmd, "br", outdir).unwrap();
}
```

### Dynamic Issue ID Completion
```bash
# For bash - dynamic completion of issue IDs
_br_complete_issues() {
    local issues=$(br list --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.issues[].id')
    COMPREPLY=($(compgen -W "$issues" -- "${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}"))
}
```

### Installation Locations
| Shell | System-wide | User |
|-------|-------------|------|
| bash | /etc/bash_completion.d/ | ~/.local/share/bash-completion/ |
| zsh | /usr/share/zsh/site-functions/ | ~/.zsh/completions/ |
| fish | /usr/share/fish/vendor_completions.d/ | ~/.config/fish/completions/ |

### Commands to Generate/Install
```bash
# Generate completions
br completions bash > br.bash
br completions zsh > _br
br completions fish > br.fish

# Install (user)
br completions --install
```

## Files to Create
- `completions/br.bash` - Bash completions
- `completions/_br` - Zsh completions  
- `completions/br.fish` - Fish completions
- `src/cli/completions.rs` - Completion command impl

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `br <TAB>` completes commands in bash/zsh/fish
- [ ] `br show <TAB>` completes issue IDs dynamically
- [ ] `br --<TAB>` completes global flags
- [ ] `br completions bash` outputs valid bash completion script
- [ ] `br completions --install` installs to correct location
- [ ] Installer script optionally installs completions
- [ ] Works without database (graceful degradation)

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_completion_script_generation() {
    let mut cmd = build_cli();
    let mut output = Vec::new();
    generate(Bash, &mut cmd, "br", &mut output);
    let script = String::from_utf8(output).unwrap();
    assert!(script.contains("complete -F"));
    assert!(script.contains("br"));
}
```

### E2E Tests
```bash
# Test completion script is valid bash
bash -n <(br completions bash)

# Test zsh completion loads
zsh -c 'source <(br completions zsh); compdef' 

# Test dynamic issue completion
br init && br create "Test" --type task
br completions bash | grep -q 'bd-'
```

### Logging
- Log completion script generation
- Log installation path used
- Log any shell detection issues","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletAnchor","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T20:20:50.947353105Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T20:33:34.802081897Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T20:33:34.802037864Z","close_reason":"Implemented shell completions for bash/zsh/fish/PowerShell/elvish using clap_complete. Features: br completions <shell> command, output to stdout or file (-o), 6 unit tests for completion generation. Completions include all commands, subcommands, and flags. Note: Dynamic issue ID completion requires shell-specific scripting that could be a follow-up enhancement.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-7z25","title":"Integrate rich output into delete command","description":"## Command: br delete <ID...>

### Traffic Level: LOW
Destructive command - rarely used but needs clear feedback.

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/delete.rs
Output: Plain text summary of deleted items

### Integration Steps
1. Show confirmation panel for dangerous operation
2. Display dry-run preview with visual diff
3. Show summary of cascade effects (deps removed, orphans created)

### Confirmation Panel (for non-force mode)
```
╭─ ⚠ Delete Issue(s) ────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  About to delete:                                   │
│  • beads_rust-abc1: Fix authentication bug          │
│  • beads_rust-def2: Update login page               │
│                                                     │
│  This will also:                                    │
│  • Remove 3 dependencies                            │
│  • Remove 5 labels                                  │
│  • Create 2 orphaned issues                         │
│                                                     │
│  [Y]es  [N]o  [D]ry-run                            │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Dry-Run Output
```
╭─ DRY RUN: Delete ──────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  Would delete:                                      │
│  ├── beads_rust-abc1  Fix authentication bug        │
│  └── beads_rust-def2  Update login page             │
│                                                     │
│  Would remove:                                      │
│  • 3 dependencies                                   │
│  • 5 labels                                         │
│  • 4 events                                         │
│                                                     │
│  Would orphan:                                      │
│  ├── beads_rust-ghi3  Child of abc1                 │
│  └── beads_rust-jkl4  Child of def2                 │
│                                                     │
│  Run without --dry-run to execute                   │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Success Output
```
✓ Deleted 2 issues

  Removed: 3 dependencies, 5 labels, 4 events
  
  ⚠ 2 issues are now orphaned:
  └── beads_rust-ghi3, beads_rust-jkl4
```

### Force Mode
With --force, skip confirmation but still show summary.

### Agent Mode (--silent)
Output only the IDs of deleted issues, one per line.

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_delete_confirmation_panel() {
        let issues = vec![make_issue("a"), make_issue("b")];
        let effects = DeleteEffects { deps: 3, labels: 5, orphans: 2 };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = render_delete_confirmation(&issues, &effects, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("Delete Issue"));
        assert!(output.contains("3 dependencies"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_delete_dry_run_output() {
        let result = DeleteResult { deleted: vec!["a".into()], ..Default::default() };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = render_dry_run(&result, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("DRY RUN"));
        assert!(output.contains("Would delete"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_delete_success_output() {
        let result = DeleteResult { deleted_count: 2, dependencies_removed: 3, ..Default::default() };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = render_delete_success(&result, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("Deleted 2"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_delete_json_output() {
        let result = DeleteResult { deleted_count: 1, ..Default::default() };
        let ctx = OutputContext::json();
        let output = format_delete_result(&result, &ctx);
        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
        assert!(parsed["deleted_count"].is_number());
    }
}
```

### E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Delete Command ==="
setup_test_db

log_step "Initialize and create test issues"
br init --prefix test
ID1=$(br create "Issue to delete" --silent)
ID2=$(br create "Another to delete" --silent)

log_step "Testing delete --dry-run"
DRY_OUTPUT=$(br delete "$ID1" --dry-run)
log_debug "Dry run: $DRY_OUTPUT"
if echo "$DRY_OUTPUT" | grep -qi "dry\|would"; then
    log_pass "Dry run shows preview"
else
    log_warn "Dry run format different"
fi

log_step "Testing actual delete"
br delete "$ID1" --force
if br show "$ID1" 2>&1 | grep -qi "not found\|deleted"; then
    log_pass "Issue deleted"
else
    log_fail "Issue still exists"
    exit 1
fi

log_step "Testing JSON output"
DELETE_JSON=$(br delete "$ID2" --force --json)
log_debug "JSON: $DELETE_JSON"
echo "$DELETE_JSON" | jq -e '.deleted_count'
log_pass "Delete JSON valid"

log_pass "=== All delete tests passed ==="
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log delete intent: `info!(issue_ids = ?ids, force, dry_run, "Processing delete command")`
- Log cascade effects: `debug!(deps, labels, events, "Calculated cascade effects")`
- Log completion: `info!(deleted_count, "Delete operation complete")`","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:46:21.388382762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:44:48.453723341Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:44:48.453666634Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-7z25","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-11n3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-7z25","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-25e5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-7z25","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-31nl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-84lm","title":"Fresh-eyes audit of representative workflow paths","description":"User-directed multi-pass audit: choose a few representative code paths, trace their execution flows through related modules, identify obvious bugs or logic mistakes with fresh eyes, and fix them methodically while following AGENTS.md rules. Use this bead to track the audit and any concrete bug fixes or follow-up beads created from it.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:26.453709987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T21:44:38.353714158Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":252,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Audit progress: traced search, dep, version, output, and sync path flows. Fixed version --check to use the shared self_update GitHub release backend instead of shelling out to curl, with explicit unsupported behavior when self_update is compiled out. Fixed is_sync_path_allowed() so it now shares the full sync-path validator instead of allowing lexically-internal symlink escapes; added a regression test for the symlink case. Verification: file-level rustfmt --check passed for src/cli/commands/version.rs, src/cli/commands/upgrade.rs, and src/sync/path.rs. Remote rch cargo check --all-targets and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings are both blocked by an upstream frankensqlite path dependency error in fsqlite-core (missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION).","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:45Z"},{"id":253,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional deep-review finding: in src/cli/commands/sync.rs, execute_merge() was saving beads.base.jsonl from report.kept before the forced export completed. That meant the ancestor snapshot could diverge from reality in two ways: (1) generated merge-note comments added to the DB after apply_merge_report() were omitted from the saved base snapshot, and (2) a later export failure could still advance the ancestor snapshot even though JSONL was never successfully updated. Fixed by snapshotting the finalized exported JSONL after finalize_export(), and added a regression test that exercises a both-modified merge and asserts the saved base snapshot includes the exported br-sync merge-note comment. Verification: rustfmt --check passes for src/cli/commands/sync.rs. Remote rch cargo check/test are currently blocked by upstream frankensqlite compile failures in fsqlite-vdbe (borrow-check E0502 in engine.rs), so the new regression test could not execute on the current dependency state.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:37:11Z"},{"id":254,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional audit finding: src/cli/commands/where.rs compared the redirect-resolved .beads directory against the raw discovery path when deciding whether to populate redirected_from. That produced false-positive redirect reports for non-canonical but equivalent --db paths like workspace/../workspace/.beads/beads.db even when no redirect file was involved. Fixed by canonicalizing the source directory before the comparison and by sharing config::MAX_REDIRECT_DEPTH instead of an inline literal. Added a regression test covering the non-canonical DB override case. Verification so far: rustfmt --check passes for src/cli/commands/where.rs. Remote rch cargo check --all-targets is still blocked by the upstream frankensqlite missing-constant error in fsqlite-core; a targeted rch cargo test for the new where regression is still compiling at the time of this note.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:48:03Z"},{"id":255,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional audit pass: fixed two more path-policy bugs. (1) src/config/mod.rs::implicit_external_jsonl_allowed() used a raw parent-directory equality check, so a non-canonical external --db path and a canonical sibling external JSONL path could be misclassified as unrelated even though they referred to the same external DB family. Fixed by normalizing parent dirs before comparison and added a regression test for the non-canonical external DB parent case. (2) src/cli/commands/history.rs used a bespoke raw-prefix external-target check instead of the shared config path classifier, so a symlinked internal JSONL target could be treated as external and incorrectly force --allow-external-jsonl in the suggested restore command. Fixed by delegating to config::resolved_jsonl_path_is_external() and added a symlink regression test. Verification so far: rustfmt --check passes for src/config/mod.rs and src/cli/commands/history.rs. Remote rch cargo check --all-targets still fails in upstream frankensqlite with missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION in fsqlite-core. Focused rch cargo tests for the new config/history regressions are still running at the time of this note.","created_at":"2026-03-25T17:25:22Z"},{"id":256,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional audit pass: fixed a deeper shared-path-classification bug and its sync callers. In src/config/mod.rs, path_is_within_beads_dir() previously fell back to the raw relative path when the target and its parent did not yet exist, so missing relative paths like .beads/nested/issues.jsonl could be misclassified as external. Fixed by anchoring unresolved relative paths to cwd and lexically normalizing them. Then updated src/sync/mod.rs preflight_export()/preflight_import() and src/cli/commands/sync.rs::validate_sync_paths() to use the shared resolved_jsonl_path_is_external() classifier instead of bespoke raw prefix checks. This also fixes symlinked internal JSONL paths that live outside .beads lexically but resolve back inside it: preflight no longer warns as if they were external, and sync CLI no longer spuriously requires --allow-external-jsonl for them. Added regressions in src/config/mod.rs, src/sync/mod.rs, and src/cli/commands/sync.rs. Verification so far: rustfmt --check passes for src/config/mod.rs, src/sync/mod.rs, and src/cli/commands/sync.rs. A fresh remote rch cargo check --all-targets is still running on a new worker; previous cargo check runs still reproduced the upstream frankensqlite missing-constant error in fsqlite-core. Fresh focused rch cargo tests for the new config/symlink regressions are still in progress at the time of this note.","created_at":"2026-03-25T17:34:43Z"},{"id":257,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional audit pass: fixed three more path-policy defects. (1) src/cli/commands/doctor.rs was treating any valid external JSONL path as fully healthy, even when the configured DB remained inside .beads and normal sync/auto-import flows would still require explicit --allow-external-jsonl opt-in. Doctor now distinguishes implicitly allowed external DB families from opt-in-only external JSONL paths, reports the latter as Warn instead of Ok, and records implicit_allow_external_jsonl plus db_path in the machine-readable details. Added doctor regressions for both the warn case and the valid external DB-family case. (2) src/sync/path.rs resolved symlink targets using read_link() output directly, which interprets relative symlink targets against the process cwd instead of the symlink parent. That could misclassify a relative internal symlink as a SymlinkEscape. Fixed by resolving relative symlink targets from the link parent before canonical/lexical normalization and added a regression that verifies a relative internal symlink is rejected as a non-regular file rather than misreported as an escape. (3) src/validation/mod.rs::SyncSafetyValidator::validate_path_containment() still used the older raw relative-path containment logic, so missing relative .beads/nested/issues.jsonl paths could be rejected even though the shared config classifier treats them as internal. Fixed by delegating to config::resolved_jsonl_path_is_external() and added a regression with a serialized cwd guard. Verification: rustfmt --check passes for src/cli/commands/doctor.rs, src/sync/path.rs, and src/validation/mod.rs. Remote rch targeted tests passed for the two doctor regressions. Fresh remote rch cargo check --all-targets and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings both still fail upstream in frankensqlite/fsqlite-core on missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION. The two new focused rch tests for the symlink and validation regressions remained running without a final result at the time of this note, so they are currently inconclusive rather than counted as passes.","created_at":"2026-03-25T17:52:32Z"},{"id":258,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit pass: fixed MCP server request handling so tools/resources/prompts now reopen full storage contexts via open_storage_with_cli instead of raw SqliteStorage::open. Root cause was MCP bypassing no-db/recovery/config precedence, which made no-db reads incorrect and could drop no-db mutations without flushing JSONL. Added regressions in src/mcp/mod.rs for no-db JSONL reads and no-db mutation persistence. Also fixed init to reuse an existing _beads workspace instead of creating a duplicate .beads tree, and updated the rich init layout label to show the actual workspace directory name. Verification: rustfmt --check passes for src/mcp/{mod,tools,resources,prompts}.rs and src/cli/commands/init.rs. Current rch jobs: cargo check --features mcp --lib plus focused tests for the two MCP regressions and the new init regression are still running or silent after compile/link phase, so I am treating them as in-flight rather than as passes.","created_at":"2026-03-25T18:14:52Z"},{"id":259,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continued fresh-eyes workspace audit. Fixed an incomplete doctor.rs refactor and then found another concrete _beads bug family: doctor still emitted .beads-specific remediation strings even when the active workspace was _beads, so merge-artifact warnings, external-JSONL guidance, symlink-escape warnings, and missing-JSONL diagnostics could point operators at the wrong directory name. doctor.rs now derives those messages from the resolved workspace marker and includes new regressions for merge artifacts, external JSONL remediation, and missing JSONL messaging under _beads. I also cleaned up two lower-severity but real UX mismatches: config now labels the project layer as 'project config' instead of '.beads/config', and the AGENTS blurb now says issues live in the project beads workspace rather than hardcoding . Verification so far: rustfmt --check passes for doctor.rs, config.rs, and agents.rs; earlier targeted rch tests for the first two _beads doctor regressions both passed; fresh targeted rch tests for the new doctor/config/agents regressions are still compiling/in flight. cargo clippy --all-targets still fails upstream in frankensqlite because fsqlite-core references missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION.","created_at":"2026-03-25T18:27:42Z"},{"id":260,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Follow-up correction: the previous audit comment was shell-quoted poorly and truncated the literal workspace path marker. The intended note is that the AGENTS blurb no longer hardcodes the  path; it now uses workspace-neutral wording so  projects are described correctly too.","created_at":"2026-03-25T18:28:27Z"},{"id":261,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Correction to the previous note: the AGENTS blurb no longer hardcodes the .beads path. It now uses workspace-neutral wording so both .beads and _beads projects are described correctly.","created_at":"2026-03-25T18:28:35Z"},{"id":262,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continued history/sync audit. Fixed restore next-step routing so the suggested command now preserves explicit --db context, distinguishes persistent JSONL routing from transient BEADS_JSONL overrides, and stays self-contained for env-driven custom/external paths. Also normalized history backup target identity so non-canonical internal paths no longer fork backup dedup/rotation lineage. Added inline regressions in src/cli/commands/history.rs, src/sync/history.rs, and src/config/mod.rs. Remote cargo check via rch still blocked upstream in frankensqlite on missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION; several focused rch tests remain in flight/inconclusive due remote queue contention.","created_at":"2026-03-25T18:54:27Z"},{"id":263,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Follow-up audit fixes: (1) changelog now prefers the workspace repo over an external JSONL repo when resolving git tags/commits, matching orphans semantics; added repo-root regressions in src/cli/commands/changelog.rs. (2) stats::execute_with_storage now resolves the active JSONL path from startup config instead of hardcoding .beads/issues.jsonl when only a storage handle is preloaded; added regression in src/cli/commands/stats.rs. Verified with rch cargo test resolve_repo_root_ --lib -- --nocapture (4 repo-root tests passed) and rch cargo test resolve_active_jsonl_path_uses_startup_config_without_storage_ctx --lib -- --nocapture (1 stats test passed). Also corrected an earlier remote-test invocation mistake: short-name filters plus --exact had compiled the crate but matched zero tests.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:07:12Z"},{"id":264,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Additional audit fixes: (1) graph no longer reopens and ignores a caller-provided routed storage context for external routes; execute() now uses the storage it already opened, and execute_with_storage_ctx only reopens when the provided context targets a different beads dir. Added graph regressions for matching vs mismatched routed storage contexts. (2) stats recent-activity lookup now canonicalizes the active JSONL path before deriving git repo/pathspec, so symlinked JSONL targets resolve against the real tracked file instead of the symlink location. Added a unix symlink regression. Verified with rch cargo test storage_ctx_targets_beads_dir --lib -- --nocapture (2 passed), rch cargo test symlinked_jsonl --lib -- --nocapture (3 passed including the new stats regression), rch cargo check --all-targets (passed), and rch cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (passed).","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:31:45Z"},{"id":265,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fresh audit pass: fixed a compile oversight in the new show.rs regression tests by importing std::path::PathBuf; verified with 'rch exec -- cargo test batch_uses_preloaded_storage --lib -- --nocapture' (2 tests passed). Found and fixed two more path/routing bugs: (1) sync/path.rs require_safe_sync_overwrite_path() was still using a raw starts_with() shortcut, so noncanonical internal .beads paths could be misclassified as external; now only OutsideBeadsDir falls through to external validation, and other internal validator failures remain terminal. Added test_safe_overwrite_accepts_normalized_internal_path. (2) config/routing.rs resolve_route() returned the raw local .beads path for unmatched/local IDs while routed self-references returned normalized paths; with a noncanonical local beads dir this could split one project into multiple route batches and change batched mutator semantics. Now local/unmatched routes normalize the local beads dir up front, town-level self-comparisons use the normalized path, and added group_issue_inputs_by_route_merges_noncanonical_local_and_self_route_batches. Remote verification for the new routing regression is currently blocked by upstream frankensqlite compile break in fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs (missing InSet import), so only rustfmt is currently green for the new routing/sync changes.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:53:31Z"},{"id":266,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Additional fresh-eyes fixes: (1) doctor.rs now treats a noncanonical configured issues.jsonl path that still resolves to the default workspace export as equivalent, so doctor fallback/diagnostics no longer miss the workspace JSONL just because the path spelling differs; added select_doctor_jsonl_path_falls_back_for_noncanonical_default_jsonl_path. (2) audit.rs execute_summary now uses open_storage_ctx_with_auto_import instead of opening storage directly, so audit summary no longer bypasses the standard stale-JSONL auto-import path. (3) audit.rs runtime panic surfaces tightened: append_entry no longer uses expect() to recover the generated ID after append, and event_type_style no longer unwraps static Color::parse results at runtime, using theme styles instead. rustfmt --check is green for audit.rs, doctor.rs, config/routing.rs, sync/path.rs, and show.rs. Focused verification passed for sync::path::tests::test_safe_overwrite_accepts_normalized_internal_path. The show.rs focused routed-storage tests remain green. The doctor regression test is still in-flight/no-output from rch as of this comment, so not counted as a pass yet.","created_at":"2026-03-25T20:00:41Z"},{"id":267,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fixed no-db config inspection drift: config list/get now suppress DB-backed runtime config when effective no-db is active, and where/info no longer prefer DB prefix inference over JSONL when no-db is enabled. Verified with rustfmt --check on config/where/info plus rch cargo test prefers_jsonl_prefix_when --lib -- --nocapture, rch cargo test test_build_layers_skips_db_layer --lib -- --nocapture, rch cargo check --all-targets, and rch cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-03-25T21:18:54Z"},{"id":268,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fixed another no-db inspection bug in info: when effective no-db was active, info still serialized issue_count/config/schema from the on-disk DB snapshot, which could report stale state as live. collect_info_output now suppresses DB-backed snapshot fields in no-db mode. Verified with rustfmt --check on info/where/config, rch cargo test test_collect_info_output_omits_db_snapshot_fields_when_cli_no_db_is_true --lib -- --nocapture, and rch cargo check --all-targets. Full rch cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings is currently blocked upstream in /data/projects/frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs:12171 with an unrelated E0308 mismatched-types error.","created_at":"2026-03-25T21:37:52Z"},{"id":269,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up: fixed a stale info regression test that still expected DB-backed config details in startup no-db mode after the no-db snapshot suppression change. Verified with rustfmt --check src/cli/commands/info.rs and rch cargo test collect_info_output_ --lib -- --nocapture (10 info tests passed).","created_at":"2026-03-25T21:44:38Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-84lm","title":"Fresh-eyes audit of representative workflow paths","description":"User-directed multi-pass audit: choose a few representative code paths, trace their execution flows through related modules, identify obvious bugs or logic mistakes with fresh eyes, and fix them methodically while following AGENTS.md rules. Use this bead to track the audit and any concrete bug fixes or follow-up beads created from it.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:26.453709987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T23:06:16.270736047Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":252,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Audit progress: traced search, dep, version, output, and sync path flows. Fixed version --check to use the shared self_update GitHub release backend instead of shelling out to curl, with explicit unsupported behavior when self_update is compiled out. Fixed is_sync_path_allowed() so it now shares the full sync-path validator instead of allowing lexically-internal symlink escapes; added a regression test for the symlink case. Verification: file-level rustfmt --check passed for src/cli/commands/version.rs, src/cli/commands/upgrade.rs, and src/sync/path.rs. Remote rch cargo check --all-targets and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings are both blocked by an upstream frankensqlite path dependency error in fsqlite-core (missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION).","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:45Z"},{"id":253,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional deep-review finding: in src/cli/commands/sync.rs, execute_merge() was saving beads.base.jsonl from report.kept before the forced export completed. That meant the ancestor snapshot could diverge from reality in two ways: (1) generated merge-note comments added to the DB after apply_merge_report() were omitted from the saved base snapshot, and (2) a later export failure could still advance the ancestor snapshot even though JSONL was never successfully updated. Fixed by snapshotting the finalized exported JSONL after finalize_export(), and added a regression test that exercises a both-modified merge and asserts the saved base snapshot includes the exported br-sync merge-note comment. Verification: rustfmt --check passes for src/cli/commands/sync.rs. Remote rch cargo check/test are currently blocked by upstream frankensqlite compile failures in fsqlite-vdbe (borrow-check E0502 in engine.rs), so the new regression test could not execute on the current dependency state.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:37:11Z"},{"id":254,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional audit finding: src/cli/commands/where.rs compared the redirect-resolved .beads directory against the raw discovery path when deciding whether to populate redirected_from. That produced false-positive redirect reports for non-canonical but equivalent --db paths like workspace/../workspace/.beads/beads.db even when no redirect file was involved. Fixed by canonicalizing the source directory before the comparison and by sharing config::MAX_REDIRECT_DEPTH instead of an inline literal. Added a regression test covering the non-canonical DB override case. Verification so far: rustfmt --check passes for src/cli/commands/where.rs. Remote rch cargo check --all-targets is still blocked by the upstream frankensqlite missing-constant error in fsqlite-core; a targeted rch cargo test for the new where regression is still compiling at the time of this note.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:48:03Z"},{"id":255,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional audit pass: fixed two more path-policy bugs. (1) src/config/mod.rs::implicit_external_jsonl_allowed() used a raw parent-directory equality check, so a non-canonical external --db path and a canonical sibling external JSONL path could be misclassified as unrelated even though they referred to the same external DB family. Fixed by normalizing parent dirs before comparison and added a regression test for the non-canonical external DB parent case. (2) src/cli/commands/history.rs used a bespoke raw-prefix external-target check instead of the shared config path classifier, so a symlinked internal JSONL target could be treated as external and incorrectly force --allow-external-jsonl in the suggested restore command. Fixed by delegating to config::resolved_jsonl_path_is_external() and added a symlink regression test. Verification so far: rustfmt --check passes for src/config/mod.rs and src/cli/commands/history.rs. Remote rch cargo check --all-targets still fails in upstream frankensqlite with missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION in fsqlite-core. Focused rch cargo tests for the new config/history regressions are still running at the time of this note.","created_at":"2026-03-25T17:25:22Z"},{"id":256,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional audit pass: fixed a deeper shared-path-classification bug and its sync callers. In src/config/mod.rs, path_is_within_beads_dir() previously fell back to the raw relative path when the target and its parent did not yet exist, so missing relative paths like .beads/nested/issues.jsonl could be misclassified as external. Fixed by anchoring unresolved relative paths to cwd and lexically normalizing them. Then updated src/sync/mod.rs preflight_export()/preflight_import() and src/cli/commands/sync.rs::validate_sync_paths() to use the shared resolved_jsonl_path_is_external() classifier instead of bespoke raw prefix checks. This also fixes symlinked internal JSONL paths that live outside .beads lexically but resolve back inside it: preflight no longer warns as if they were external, and sync CLI no longer spuriously requires --allow-external-jsonl for them. Added regressions in src/config/mod.rs, src/sync/mod.rs, and src/cli/commands/sync.rs. Verification so far: rustfmt --check passes for src/config/mod.rs, src/sync/mod.rs, and src/cli/commands/sync.rs. A fresh remote rch cargo check --all-targets is still running on a new worker; previous cargo check runs still reproduced the upstream frankensqlite missing-constant error in fsqlite-core. Fresh focused rch cargo tests for the new config/symlink regressions are still in progress at the time of this note.","created_at":"2026-03-25T17:34:43Z"},{"id":257,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"BronzeIvy","text":"Additional audit pass: fixed three more path-policy defects. (1) src/cli/commands/doctor.rs was treating any valid external JSONL path as fully healthy, even when the configured DB remained inside .beads and normal sync/auto-import flows would still require explicit --allow-external-jsonl opt-in. Doctor now distinguishes implicitly allowed external DB families from opt-in-only external JSONL paths, reports the latter as Warn instead of Ok, and records implicit_allow_external_jsonl plus db_path in the machine-readable details. Added doctor regressions for both the warn case and the valid external DB-family case. (2) src/sync/path.rs resolved symlink targets using read_link() output directly, which interprets relative symlink targets against the process cwd instead of the symlink parent. That could misclassify a relative internal symlink as a SymlinkEscape. Fixed by resolving relative symlink targets from the link parent before canonical/lexical normalization and added a regression that verifies a relative internal symlink is rejected as a non-regular file rather than misreported as an escape. (3) src/validation/mod.rs::SyncSafetyValidator::validate_path_containment() still used the older raw relative-path containment logic, so missing relative .beads/nested/issues.jsonl paths could be rejected even though the shared config classifier treats them as internal. Fixed by delegating to config::resolved_jsonl_path_is_external() and added a regression with a serialized cwd guard. Verification: rustfmt --check passes for src/cli/commands/doctor.rs, src/sync/path.rs, and src/validation/mod.rs. Remote rch targeted tests passed for the two doctor regressions. Fresh remote rch cargo check --all-targets and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings both still fail upstream in frankensqlite/fsqlite-core on missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION. The two new focused rch tests for the symlink and validation regressions remained running without a final result at the time of this note, so they are currently inconclusive rather than counted as passes.","created_at":"2026-03-25T17:52:32Z"},{"id":258,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit pass: fixed MCP server request handling so tools/resources/prompts now reopen full storage contexts via open_storage_with_cli instead of raw SqliteStorage::open. Root cause was MCP bypassing no-db/recovery/config precedence, which made no-db reads incorrect and could drop no-db mutations without flushing JSONL. Added regressions in src/mcp/mod.rs for no-db JSONL reads and no-db mutation persistence. Also fixed init to reuse an existing _beads workspace instead of creating a duplicate .beads tree, and updated the rich init layout label to show the actual workspace directory name. Verification: rustfmt --check passes for src/mcp/{mod,tools,resources,prompts}.rs and src/cli/commands/init.rs. Current rch jobs: cargo check --features mcp --lib plus focused tests for the two MCP regressions and the new init regression are still running or silent after compile/link phase, so I am treating them as in-flight rather than as passes.","created_at":"2026-03-25T18:14:52Z"},{"id":259,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continued fresh-eyes workspace audit. Fixed an incomplete doctor.rs refactor and then found another concrete _beads bug family: doctor still emitted .beads-specific remediation strings even when the active workspace was _beads, so merge-artifact warnings, external-JSONL guidance, symlink-escape warnings, and missing-JSONL diagnostics could point operators at the wrong directory name. doctor.rs now derives those messages from the resolved workspace marker and includes new regressions for merge artifacts, external JSONL remediation, and missing JSONL messaging under _beads. I also cleaned up two lower-severity but real UX mismatches: config now labels the project layer as 'project config' instead of '.beads/config', and the AGENTS blurb now says issues live in the project beads workspace rather than hardcoding . Verification so far: rustfmt --check passes for doctor.rs, config.rs, and agents.rs; earlier targeted rch tests for the first two _beads doctor regressions both passed; fresh targeted rch tests for the new doctor/config/agents regressions are still compiling/in flight. cargo clippy --all-targets still fails upstream in frankensqlite because fsqlite-core references missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION.","created_at":"2026-03-25T18:27:42Z"},{"id":260,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Follow-up correction: the previous audit comment was shell-quoted poorly and truncated the literal workspace path marker. The intended note is that the AGENTS blurb no longer hardcodes the  path; it now uses workspace-neutral wording so  projects are described correctly too.","created_at":"2026-03-25T18:28:27Z"},{"id":261,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Correction to the previous note: the AGENTS blurb no longer hardcodes the .beads path. Fixed restore next-step routing so the suggested command now preserves explicit --db context, distinguishes persistent JSONL routing from transient BEADS_JSONL overrides, and stays self-contained for env-driven custom/external paths. Also normalized history backup target identity so non-canonical internal paths no longer fork backup dedup/rotation lineage. Added inline regressions in src/cli/commands/history.rs, src/sync/history.rs, and src/config/mod.rs. Remote cargo check via rch still blocked upstream in frankensqlite on missing fsqlite_types::FRANKENSQLITE_SQLITE_VERSION; several focused rch tests remain in flight/inconclusive due remote queue contention.","created_at":"2026-03-25T18:54:27Z"},{"id":263,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Follow-up audit fixes: (1) changelog now prefers the workspace repo over an external JSONL repo when resolving git tags/commits, matching orphans semantics; added repo-root regressions in src/cli/commands/changelog.rs. (2) stats::execute_with_storage now resolves the active JSONL path from startup config instead of hardcoding .beads/issues.jsonl when only a storage handle is preloaded; added regression in src/cli/commands/stats.rs. Verified with rch cargo test resolve_repo_root_ --lib -- --nocapture (4 repo-root tests passed) and rch cargo test resolve_active_jsonl_path_uses_startup_config_without_storage_ctx --lib -- --nocapture (1 stats test passed). (2) config/routing.rs resolve_route() returned the raw local .beads path for unmatched/local IDs while routed self-references returned normalized paths; with a noncanonical local beads dir this could split one project into multiple route batches and change batched mutator semantics. Now local/unmatched routes normalize the local beads dir up front, town-level self-comparisons use the normalized path, and added group_issue_inputs_by_route_merges_noncanonical_local_and_self_route_batches. Remote verification for the new routing regression is currently blocked by upstream frankensqlite compile break in fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs (missing InSet import), so only rustfmt is currently green for the new routing/sync changes.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:53:31Z"},{"id":266,"issue_id":"beads_rust-84lm","author":"ubuntu","text":"Additional fresh-eyes fixes: (1) doctor.rs now treats a noncanonical configured issues.jsonl path that still resolves to the default workspace export as equivalent, so doctor fallback/diagnostics no longer miss the workspace JSONL just because the path spelling differs; added select_doctor_jsonl_path_falls_back_for_noncanonical_default_jsonl_path.  {"id":"beads_rust-8cc","title":"version Command (build metadata output)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:04:47.540281686Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:02.266364655Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:02.266364655Z","close_reason":"Superseded by beads_rust-k8p (version command spec)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-8f8","title":"EPIC: Port beads (SQLite+JSONL) to Rust as 'br'","description":"# EPIC: Port beads (SQLite + JSONL) to Rust (`br`)

## Purpose
Deliver a classic, non-invasive Rust port of bd with full JSONL/SQLite parity and command compatibility. This epic coordinates phases and enforces scope boundaries.

## In-Scope (classic parity)
- Core CRUD + query commands
- SQLite schema compatibility
- JSONL import/export + auto-flush/import
- ID generation (base36 adaptive) + content hashing
- Config system (YAML + DB) + metadata.json
- Blocked cache + ready/blocked semantics
- Output parity (JSON shapes + golden text output)
- Comprehensive tests + conformance harness

## Out-of-Scope (v1)
- Daemon/RPC, git hooks/merge drivers, auto git ops
- Gastown features (agent/molecule/gate/rig/convoy/HOP)
- Linear/Jira integrations

## Success Criteria
- `br` produces identical JSON outputs to `bd` for classic commands.
- SQLite schema compatible; bd/br can share `.beads/`.
- Tests + conformance suite green.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"WildDog","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:09:37.236443424Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T06:57:04.278090021Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T06:57:04.278090021Z","close_reason":"All children completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-8f8.1","title":"Auto-import on read commands","description":"Implement classic bd auto-import before read-only commands. Use staleness detection + hash from sync/status, honor --no-auto-import (error) and --allow-stale (warn + skip). Import should be safe (skip prefix validation) and avoid .git; must not run for mutating commands.","notes":"Added E2E coverage in tests/e2e_sync_artifacts.rs: auto-import on read (list) when JSONL newer, --allow-stale skips, --no-auto-import errors. Verified with cargo fmt/check + clippy + check.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildDog","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T21:46:55.759692064Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:38:23.946813246Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:38:23.946813246Z","close_reason":"Auto-import implemented in main.rs + sync::auto_import_if_stale; E2E coverage added in tests/e2e_sync_artifacts.rs; reviewed complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-8f8.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-8f8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":100,"issue_id":"beads_rust-8f8.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reviewed implementation: main.rs uses should_auto_import + run_auto_import for read-only commands; sync::auto_import_if_stale checks staleness + jsonl hash, honors --allow-stale (warn+skip) and --no-auto-import (error), uses ImportConfig with skip_prefix_validation=true and beads_dir path validation (no external JSONL). Looks complete per spec; suggest closing after quick manual validation.","created_at":"2026-01-18T00:42:09Z"}]}
